metadata.description: Challenges validating timeout enforcement and recovery behavior across all 8 protocol services: HttpTimeout plugin configuration, connection/request/socket timeouts, coroutine cancellation propagation, retry-after-timeout, and recovery from partial operations interrupted by timeout

challenges.name: HttpTimeout Plugin Installed on All Platforms
challenges.description: Verify that the HttpTimeout plugin is installed in the HttpClient factory on all 4 platforms (Android, Desktop, iOS, Wasm). For each platform, check that connectTimeoutMillis, requestTimeoutMillis, and socketTimeoutMillis are configured with non-zero values. A missing timeout configuration means operations can hang indefinitely.

outputs.name: platforms_with_timeout
outputs.description: Map of platform to whether HttpTimeout is installed

outputs.name: platforms_missing_timeout
outputs.description: Platforms where HttpTimeout is not configured

outputs.name: timeout_values
outputs.description: connectTimeoutMillis/requestTimeoutMillis/socketTimeoutMillis per platform

assertions.message: All platforms must have HttpTimeout installed in HttpClient
HttpTimeout must be installed on Android, Desktop, iOS, and Wasm

challenges.name: Connection Timeout Enforcement Across All Protocols
challenges.description: Verify that all 8 protocol services (Dropbox, GoogleDrive, OneDrive, FTP, SFTP, SMB, WebDAV, S3) enforce connection timeouts when connecting to an unreachable endpoint. Configure a 3-second connection timeout, attempt to connect to a black-hole address (203.0.113.1), and verify each service throws a timeout exception within 3 seconds + 500ms tolerance. No service may block indefinitely.

outputs.name: timeout_results
outputs.description: Connection timeout results per protocol (timed_out, actual_ms, exception_type)

outputs.name: hanging_services
outputs.description: Services that exceeded the timeout window

outputs.name: exception_types
outputs.description: Exception thrown on timeout per service

outputs.name: all_enforced
outputs.description: Whether all services enforced the connection timeout

assertions.message: No protocol service may hang beyond the configured connection timeout
All 8 protocol services must enforce connection timeout
All 8 protocol services must be tested

challenges.name: Request Timeout Enforcement During Active Transfer
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services enforce request-level timeouts for in-progress data transfers. Establish a connection to a mock server that starts sending data but then stalls mid-transfer. With a 5-second request timeout configured, the transfer must be interrupted and the service must return a timeout error within the deadline. Test with FTP, SFTP, and WebDAV services.

outputs.name: request_timeout_results
outputs.description: Request timeout results per protocol

outputs.name: services_hung
outputs.description: Services that did not abort stalled transfer in time

outputs.name: partial_data_returned
outputs.description: Whether any service returned partial data on timeout

assertions.message: No service may hang on a stalled mid-transfer request
All services must enforce request timeout during stalled transfers

challenges.name: Socket Read Timeout During Idle Connection
challenges.description: Verify that socket-level read timeouts fire when a connected server goes silent. Establish a successful connection, begin a read operation, then have the server stop sending data without closing the connection (simulating a silent server hang). With socketTimeoutMillis configured, the read must fail within the deadline.

outputs.name: socket_timeout_fired
outputs.description: Whether the socket read timeout fired

outputs.name: actual_timeout_ms
outputs.description: Actual time elapsed before timeout exception

outputs.name: timeout_exception_type
outputs.description: Type of exception thrown on socket timeout

assertions.message: Socket read timeout must fire on silent server
Socket timeout must fire within configured window plus 1 second tolerance

challenges.name: Coroutine Cancellation Propagates Through Timeout
challenges.description: Verify that when a protocol operation times out, the resulting CancellationException propagates correctly through the coroutine hierarchy without being swallowed. Launch a file download in a child coroutine with a timeout, let it time out, and verify: (1) CancellationException is rethrown not swallowed, (2) parent coroutine is notified, (3) structured concurrency scope is not corrupted, (4) subsequent operations on the same service succeed.

outputs.name: cancellation_propagated
outputs.description: Whether CancellationException propagated to parent coroutine

outputs.name: exception_swallowed
outputs.description: Whether CancellationException was caught and not rethrown

outputs.name: scope_intact_after_timeout
outputs.description: Whether coroutine scope remains usable after timeout

outputs.name: subsequent_op_succeeded
outputs.description: Whether a subsequent operation on the service succeeded after timeout

assertions.message: CancellationException must propagate through timeout to parent coroutine
CancellationException must never be swallowed in timeout catch blocks
Coroutine scope must remain intact after timeout cancellation
Service must accept new operations after timeout recovery

challenges.name: Automatic Retry After Timeout with Exponential Backoff
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services automatically retry operations after a timeout, using exponential backoff to avoid thundering herd. Simulate a server that times out the first 2 requests but succeeds on the 3rd. Verify: (1) exactly the configured retry count is attempted, (2) wait intervals double between retries, (3) the operation ultimately succeeds, (4) the caller receives the successful result.

outputs.name: retry_count
outputs.description: Number of retry attempts before success

outputs.name: retry_intervals_ms
outputs.description: Wait durations between retry attempts

outputs.name: operation_succeeded
outputs.description: Whether the operation ultimately succeeded

outputs.name: backoff_correct
outputs.description: Whether intervals follow exponential doubling pattern

assertions.message: At least one retry must be attempted after timeout
Operation must succeed after retries when server recovers
Retry intervals must follow exponential backoff pattern

challenges.name: Recovery from Partial Upload Interrupted by Timeout
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services handle partial upload interruption due to timeout gracefully. Begin a file upload to FTP or WebDAV, allow it to partially complete, then trigger a timeout. Verify: (1) the service correctly reports the operation as failed, (2) no partial file is left in a corrupted state that blocks future uploads, (3) a retry upload of the same file succeeds completely.

outputs.name: partial_upload_reported_as_failure
outputs.description: Whether the interrupted upload was correctly reported as failed

outputs.name: corrupted_file_present
outputs.description: Whether a corrupted partial file remains after timeout

outputs.name: retry_upload_succeeded
outputs.description: Whether a full retry upload succeeded after the partial failure

outputs.name: recovery_results
outputs.description: Per-protocol partial upload recovery results

assertions.message: Interrupted uploads must be reported as failed, not partially successful
No corrupted partial file may remain after upload timeout
A retry upload must succeed after the partial failure is cleaned up

challenges.name: Service State Consistency After Timeout
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services maintain consistent internal state after an operation times out. After a timeout, check: (1) isConnected() returns the correct value (not stale true if connection was dropped), (2) the service scope is not cancelled, (3) pending operations are not left in an unresolvable state, (4) the service accepts new connect() calls without throwing. Test all 8 protocol services.

outputs.name: state_consistency_results
outputs.description: State consistency check results per protocol after timeout

outputs.name: stale_connected_state
outputs.description: Services where isConnected() returned stale true after timeout

outputs.name: scope_cancelled
outputs.description: Services where serviceScope was cancelled by timeout

outputs.name: reconnect_accepted
outputs.description: Services that accepted a new connect() after timeout

assertions.message: No service may return stale connected state after a timeout that dropped the connection
Service scope must not be cancelled by a single operation timeout
All services must accept a new connect() call after timeout recovery

challenges.name: Concurrent Operation Timeout Isolation
challenges.description: Verify that a timeout in one concurrent operation does not cancel or corrupt other concurrent operations on the same service. Launch 10 file read operations concurrently on FTP or SFTP. Cause operations 1, 3, and 5 to time out. Verify that operations 2, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10 complete successfully with correct data, and that the timed-out operations only affect their own coroutines.

outputs.name: successful_ops
outputs.description: Number of concurrent operations that completed successfully

outputs.name: timed_out_ops
outputs.description: Number of operations that timed out as intended

outputs.name: unintended_failures
outputs.description: Number of operations that failed without being in the timeout set

outputs.name: isolation_maintained
outputs.description: Whether timed-out operations did not affect successful ones

assertions.message: No operation outside the timeout set may fail due to another operation timing out
Timeout isolation must be maintained across concurrent operations
At least 7 of 10 concurrent operations must succeed (the non-timeout ones)

challenges.name: Timeout Events Recorded in PerformanceMetrics
challenges.description: Verify that timeout events are captured in PerformanceMetrics so that operations teams can detect and diagnose timeout spikes. Trigger 5 connection timeouts and 5 request timeouts, then verify that PerformanceMetrics contains timeout counter increments for each event. Check that timeout duration histograms reflect accurate measurements.

outputs.name: timeout_events_recorded
outputs.description: Number of timeout events recorded in PerformanceMetrics

outputs.name: expected_timeout_count
outputs.description: Expected number of timeout events

outputs.name: metrics_accurate
outputs.description: Whether PerformanceMetrics timeout count matches expected

assertions.message: PerformanceMetrics must record all timeout events accurately
All 10 timeout events (5 connection + 5 request) must be recorded
